steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: After gaining access to records from previously missing regions, researchers took action on a shortcoming in their data coverage. The blank needs a word meaning to improve or remedy a problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — postulate: means to propose a theory. Researchers are fixing a data gap, not proposing one. ✗
  • Choice B — ameliorate: means to make something better or less severe. Filling a geographic gap in coverage improves it — fits exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — sanction: means to authorize or penalize. Neither sense applies to remedying missing data. ✗
  • Choice D — rationalize: means to explain away or justify. Researchers are correcting the gap, not excusing it. ✗
That matches Choice B — ameliorate.
